title: pedicularis thamnophila subsp. cupuliformis
intro: Pedicularis thamnophila subsp. cupuliformis, also known as the Mountain Lover's Flower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to help alleviate digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support immune function. The herbal preparations of Pedicularis thamnophila subsp. cupuliformis typically involve drying the plant material and making teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
